title: Standalone 'verify someone else's claim' CLI: re-derive final hash from any published pi dataset + manifest
context: PLAN.md v0.2 adds a new M5 deliverable: a standalone 'verify someone else's claim' CLI that takes any published pi dataset + manifest and re-derives the final hash, described as the seed of verification-as-a-service. This is distinct from the project's internal verification harness (verify-004, verify-009): it is an outward-facing tool that can validate third-party pi claims using bigger-pi's open reproducibility infrastructure. It also anchors the 'verification-as-a-service' adjacent opportunity listed in v0.2. Depends on release-018 and verify-004.
objective: Build a standalone CLI that takes any published pi digit dataset and ReleaseManifest as input and re-derives the deterministic final hash, enabling independent parties to verify third-party pi claims using bigger-pi's open infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts a published pi digit dataset and a ReleaseManifest as input and re-derives the finalResultHash using only the public ledger data and the repro command, matching the CLI interface described in PLAN.md.

The CLI reports clearly pass/fail with the computed hash vs. the claimed hash; it can validate both bigger-pi's own published results and third-party claims that conform to the ReleaseManifest schema.
No secrets, API keys, or PII required or stored; all inputs are public artifacts.
Ships with documentation and an example walkthrough re-verifying a known published pi result; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR adding the standalone 'verify someone else's claim' CLI with documentation and example walkthrough.
